scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (D, F#, A) plus safe extensions (E, B). The most foolproof scale for D major improvisation. Zero avoid notes.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (D) and major 3rd (F#) for strong resolution. The B (13th) adds brightness. Perfect for melodic hooks.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for D major in rock, country, blues, and pop. Guaranteed consonance. Ideal for memorable melodies and beginner improvisation.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental D major scale with two sharps (F#, C#). Contains all chord tones and available extensions for complete melodic freedom.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. The G (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to F# or A for smoothness. Emphasize chord tones on strong beats.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over D major. Essential for compositional development, classical approaches, and full diatonic expression in D.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises G to G# (#11) creating dreamy, ethereal quality. The characteristic Lydian sound - bright, floating, modern.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (G#) for signature Lydian color. Creates uplifting, optimistic quality. Perfect for sustained I major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Dmaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Essential for film scores, jazz, fusion, and progressive rock. Creates magical, floating quality.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lowers C# to C (b7) creating dominant 7th character. Perfect for bluesy, rock-oriented D major. The I7 sound common in folk rock.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The C (b7) creates bluesy edge. Emphasize F# and C for dominant character. Great for rock and folk guitar.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for D7 chords and folk-rock vamps. Essential for jam bands, Grateful Dead-style music, and Americana. Use when you want major with bluesy flavor.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Built on V - creates bluesy major sound
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes diatonic to D major. The minor pentatonic from the V creates safe blues-rock sound over I major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Creates bluesy major without clash. The C adds slight tension. Classic rock guitar approach to major chords.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential rock guitar scale over D major. Safe and bluesy. Perfect for rock solos in major keys.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The C# is a chord tone (maj7), making D Ionian the natural choice for Dmaj7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (C#) for sophisticated color. The G (11th) can be used but resolve to F# or A. All tones work beautifully.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Dmaj7. The quintessential maj7 scale. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B, and contemporary music.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (G#) avoids suspended sound. This is THE scale for Imaj7 in modern jazz and fusion.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (G#) is signature Lydian sound. Creates floating, dreamy quality. Essential for Dmaj7#11 voicings.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz, fusion, film music, and progressive styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Omits 4th and 7th for core consonance. Simpler maj7 approach perfect for melodic clarity.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and melodic. Implies maj7 without stating it. Great for hooks and singable lines.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for pop and R&B maj7 approaches. Creates smooth melodies. Ideal for vocal lines.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E Dorian is the natural ii mode in D major. The major 6th (C#) creates brighter, jazzier minor sound essential for ii chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the C# (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I progressions (Em7-A7-Dmaj7).
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Essential for jazz, pop, R&B, and neo-soul.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural minor with C instead of C#. Creates traditional, somber minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The C (b13) creates darker color. Use for traditional sad minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. More traditional than Dorian.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Safe minor pentatonic removes complexity. Universal minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Very safe and simple. Perfect for straightforward minor melodies.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for simple, bluesy ii minor. Works in rock, blues, and pop.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. The C# (13th) is essential for ii-V-I. The standard jazz iim7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The C# (13th) is crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to A7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for Em7-A7-Dmaj7. The definitive iim7 scale in jazz, R&B, neo-soul.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Thinking in D major reinforces key center. All notes work since Em7 is diatonic to D.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize E as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for key center emphasis. Great for teaching diatonic relationships.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: F# Phrygian is the iii mode of D major. The b2 (G) creates Spanish/exotic character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (G) for Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor. Creates drama in D major.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: All D major notes work over F#m since it's diatonic. Maintains key center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize F# as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over iii. Simple diatonic approach.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: G major is the natural scale for IV in D major. Creates subdominant function.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root (G) and major 3rd (B). The C (11th) can sound suspended.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Perfect for folk, rock, country, and pop.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (C#) creates modern, floating IV sound. Common in jazz and film music.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (C#) creates bright, dreamy quality. Perfect for Gmaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV sound. Perfect for jazz, fusion, and R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D major scale over IV maintains key center. All notes work since G is IV of D.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize G as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for diatonic IV chord approach. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in D major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: A Mixolydian shares all notes with D major. The b7 (G) creates dominant 7th character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The G (b7) creates dominant pull to D. Emphasize C# and G for strong V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in D major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major provides pure V triad. The G# (maj7) is brighter than Mixolydian's G.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More classical V approach. The G# creates major 7th rather than dominant 7th.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical, traditional, or pure major V sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D major contains all V chord tones. A Mixolydian = D Ionian starting on A.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize A as root. The G creates natural dominant character.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic V approach. Maintains key center awareness.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. The b7 (G) is a chord tone.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd (C#) and b7 (G) - the tritone that resolves to D.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (Em7-A7-Dmaj7).

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D major over A7 maintains key center. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize A as root. The G creates dominant 7th automatically.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key awareness over V7. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds blue notes for authentic blues dominant. The b5 (Eb) creates blues tension.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use Eb (b5) as passing tone to E. Creates authentic blues sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for blues and blues-rock A7. Perfect for 12-bar blues.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with D major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: B minor is the vi of D major. Shares all seven notes. Most common minor chord in D major progressions.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All D major notes work perfectly. Natural minor sound in the key.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in D major. Perfect for D-A-Bm-G, Bm-G-D-A progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Dorian adds G# (major 6th) for brighter minor. More sophisticated than natural minor.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The G# (major 6th) creates jazzy, bright minor. More modern than Aeolian.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, jazzy vi minor. Perfect for jazz, funk, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: D major and B minor share all notes. B Aeolian = D Ionian starting on B.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize B as root. All notes work perfectly.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for understanding relative major/minor. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Natural minor sound perfect for vi minor 7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. Standard vim7 sound. Perfect for emotional sections.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in D major. Perfect for D-A-Bm7-G progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple and effective. All D major notes work over Bm7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think D major, emphasize B as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic vim7 approach. Works universally.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of Western music in D major. Three-chord progression perfect for rock, folk, and country.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, optimistic, and resolute. The quintessential guitar-friendly progression.
famousSongs: Knockin' on Heaven's Door, Free Fallin', Wonderwall
commonProgressions.tips: D major is extremely guitar-friendly. All three chords are open position chords. Can enhance with D-G-A7 for more color.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop progression. The 'Axis of Awesome' progression in D major.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile. Can sound happy, sad, or nostalgic.
famousSongs: Let Her Go, With or Without You sections, countless pop hits
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely common in modern music. Works for entire songs. Can be played in any order.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic '50s progression in D. The doo-wop changes.
famousSongs: Stand By Me (transposed), Every Breath You Take sections
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro vibes. Can enhance with Dmaj7-Bm7-Gmaj7-A7 for jazz flavor.

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: The fundamental jazz progression in D major.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated with strong resolution. The gold standard for jazz harmony.
famousSongs: Autumn Leaves sections, countless jazz standards
commonProgressions.tips: Master this for jazz. Practice with extensions: Em9-A13-Dmaj9. A7 can be altered.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic folk/pop progression with smooth voice leading.
famousSongs: Country Roads, common in folk music
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for acoustic guitar. Very singable. Can add 7ths for sophistication.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop/rock starting on vi minor in D.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact. Great for building verses.

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal rock progression in D borrowing bVII (C major).
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold, anthemic, powerful with modal flavor.
commonProgressions.tips: The C major is borrowed from D Mixolydian. Creates powerful rock sound.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Double V creates urgency.
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. Repeated V emphasizes tension and release.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic folk and country progression in D.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Stable, traditional, comforting. Perfect for storytelling.
commonProgressions.tips: Very stable - keeps returning to I. Perfect for acoustic and traditional styles.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression in D combining relative minor with ii-V-I.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Extends ii-V-I with vi start. Practice jazz voicings. Great for learning harmony.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Ascending progression in D through scale degrees.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Builds tension through ascending harmony. Creates climbing sensation.
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord (F#m) is underused but effective. Creates smooth voice leading.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-ii-V
commonProgressions.description: Jazz standard progression in D - the rhythm changes.
commonProgressions.tips: Essential for jazz. Strong harmonic motion. Add tensions for sophistication.
